# Electrical Permit

## Permit Details

- **Permit Number**: EP-2026-07293
- **Type**: New Residential Electrical Installation
- **Status**: Approved (issued March 3, 2026)
- **Compliance Standard**: NEC 2023 (National Electrical Code), as adopted by City of Austin effective January 1, 2025

## Contractor

- **Company**: Bright Spark Electric LLC
- **Owner**: Tom Chen
- **License**: Texas TDLR Master Electrician License #ME-88421
- **Phone**: (512) 555-0283
- **Insurance**: General liability $2M, workers' comp through Texas Mutual

Tom has been working residential projects in the Austin area for 14 years and completed the Patterson home electrical design based on Angela Torres's architectural plans. He recommended a 400-amp service panel to accommodate future EV charging and a potential pool pump.

## Scope of Work

The electrical installation covers 42 circuits across two floors, including:

- 400-amp main service panel (Eaton CH series)
- Dedicated 50-amp circuit for future EV charger in garage
- Whole-house surge protector (Eaton CHSPT2ULTRA)
- 22 GFCI-protected outlets (kitchen, bathrooms, exterior, garage)
- AFCI protection on all bedroom circuits per NEC 210.12
- Pre-wired for rooftop solar (conduit run from attic to main panel)
- Low-voltage structured wiring: Cat6A to every room, coax to living areas
- LED recessed lighting throughout (72 fixtures total)

## Estimated Cost

Bright Spark Electric quoted $25,000 for complete electrical installation including materials and labor. A $7,500 deposit was paid on March 5, with the balance due upon final inspection approval. Tom estimates the rough-in will take 8 working days, with trim-out requiring another 4 days after drywall.
